_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: GA legislature batch: josh-mclaurin (D SD-14 — WON 2026 Dem Lt. Gov runoff, now statewide nominee; progressive record), ed-harbison (D SD-15 — RETIRING, announced 2026-03-03, not running 2026), marty-harbin (R SD-16 — RFRA/constitutional-carry SPONSOR, movement conservative), mitchell-scoggins (R HD-14 — establishment R, individually-confirmed YEA on SB233/SB140/HB1105), matthew-gambill (R HD-15 — establishment R, GLA-certified, authored HB130/HB1480). State tier. Evidence-first: party-line marquee GA votes (HB481/SB202/SB319/SB233/SB140/HB1105/SB63/HB1084) cited as HARD for the recorded side; nulls where no evidence; Freedom Index polarity-inverted labels explicitly NOT scored as conservative.

set.office: State Senator (sitting · 2026 Democratic nominee for Lieutenant Governor)

profile.background: Attorney (Yale Law); GA House District 51 2019-2023, GA State Senate District 14 (Sandy Springs) since 2023. Progressive Democrat. WON the 2026-06-16 Democratic Lt. Governor runoff (~54.9%) over Nabilah Parkes; faces the GOP nominee in the Nov 3 general while serving out his Senate term to Jan 2027.
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-20 — evidence-scored from his campaign platform (own words), issue-group endorsements (Moms Demand Action, Georgia Equality), floor record, and GA party-line marquee votes. CANDIDACY UPDATE: now the statewide Democratic LG nominee, not a Senate re-election candidate. CAUTION: freedomindex.us lists polarity-inverted vote labels for him (showing 'YES' on constitutional carry etc.) — these are NOT conservative votes; primary evidence shows he spoke AGAINST constitutional carry on the floor and opposed SB140. Not scored as deviations.

josh-mclaurin@GA.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06-20. Consistent progressive (Freedom Index 8%); no conservative deviations found. Sanctity of life: campaigns to REPEAL Georgia's 6-week abortion ban, states he voted against HB481 (2019). Marriage: supports marriage equality, endorsed by Georgia Equality, opposed SB140 (2023 minor gender-care ban). Family: voted NO on the SB233 school-voucher program; opposed minor gender-care restrictions. Christian liberty: leading public critic of Georgia's RFRA (conscience-exemption law). Elections: condemns SB202 (2021) as voter suppression, defends mail-in/drop boxes. 2A: spoke AGAINST constitutional carry (SB319) on the GA House floor (2022); endorsed by Moms Demand Action. Several cells null — no documented position.

0.note: No conception/personhood affirmation found (opposite posture documented).

1.note: Campaign priority to REPEAL Georgia's 6-week abortion ban; states he voted against HB481 in 2019 ('personal health decisions should remain between patients and their doctors').

2.note: Supports IVF access; no clean position on embryonic-discard/stem-cell — null.

4.note: Pro-abortion-rights record, but no PP/NARAL/EMILY's List money or endorsement documented either way — null.

0.note: Supports marriage equality / same-sex marriage — endorsed by Georgia Equality; contrary to one-man/one-woman.

1.note: Supports same-sex marriage; longtime critic of Georgia's RFRA as discriminatory.

2.note: Opposed SB140 (2023 ban on gender procedures for minors); Senate Democrats voted as a bloc against (party-line). Documented LGBTQ ally.

4.note: Georgia Equality-endorsed; opposes conservative LGBTQ-in-policy/schools measures.

0.note: Voted NO on the Georgia Promise Scholarship / school-voucher program (SB233-era).

1.note: Opposed SB140 minor gender-care restrictions — contrary to parental-consent-on-gender-interventions framing.

2.note: No specific cited CRT/SOGI-curriculum vote/statement found.

4.note: RFRA opponent (cuts against faith-based exemptions) but no specific faith-based-adoption vote — null.

1.note: Leading public critic of Georgia's Religious Freedom Restoration Act (conscience-exemption law) — contrary to conscience exemptions.

2.note: Favors expanded Medicaid/childcare/housing spending; no balanced-budget-amendment position on this framing — null.

4.note: Left-populist anti-corporate framing, but no specific WEF/ESG position — null.

3.note: Publicly condemns SB202 (2021) as voter suppression for cutting drop boxes and mail-in access — defends mass mail-in/drop boxes, contrary to the rubric.
Favors federal Medicaid expansion and federal voting-rights enforcement (pro-federal), but no clean cited anti-state-sovereignty vote — null.

0.note: Spoke AGAINST constitutional carry (SB319) on the GA House floor in 2022, challenging Republicans during debate.

1.note: Endorsed by Moms Demand Action (gun-control group) for 2026 — supports red-flag/AWB-type restrictions, contrary.

profile.background: U.S. Marine (Vietnam, Purple Heart); GA State Senate District 15 (Columbus/Muscogee) since 1993 — 'Dean of the Senate.' Authored veterans legislation (Purple Heart State designation, Veterans Court, Georgia Military Veterans Hall of Fame, SB109 active-duty insurance). Announced 2026-03-03 he will not seek re-election; retirement effective end of the 2026 session. His son Edward D. Harbison qualified for the seat.
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-20 — evidence-scored from GA party-line marquee votes and his documented committee/veterans record. CANDIDACY UPDATE: RETIRING (announced 2026-03-03), not running 2026 — still serving out the term. Individual roll-call lines could not be pulled (legis.ga.gov/LegiScan bot-blocked), so FALSEs rest on documented party-line passage with the full Democratic caucus opposed, scored per the marquee-party-line-vote rule.

ed-harbison@GA.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06-20. Long-serving Columbus Democrat, retiring end of 2026; consistent party-line record on the GA culture-war marquee bills (no conservative crossover found): against HB481 (2019 heartbeat), SB233 (2024 vouchers), SB202 (2021 election law), and SB319 (2022 constitutional carry) — all passed on party-line votes with the Democratic caucus opposed. His distinctive record is bipartisan VETERANS/MILITARY legislation (Purple Heart Marine; Veterans Court author), not the scorecard's culture-war categories — most cells null. No PP/NARAL money documented.

1.note: HB481 (2019 heartbeat/LIFE Act) passed the Senate on a party-line vote with all Democrats opposed; Harbison served as a Democrat. Marquee party-line vote → recorded against.

4.note: No PP/NARAL/EMILY's List money documented either way — null.

0.note: No one-man/one-woman affirmation or opposition statement found.
SB233 (2024 Georgia Promise Scholarship vouchers) passed Senate 33-21 with no Democratic support; Harbison a Democrat → recorded against school choice.

3.note: SB202 (2021 Election Integrity Act, drop-box/mail-in restrictions) passed Senate 34-20 party-line with Democrats opposed; Harbison a Democrat → recorded against the restrictions.

0.note: Veterans/military committee record, but no documented police-funding/anti-defund vote — null.
SB319 (2022 constitutional/permitless carry) passed Senate 34-22 party-line with Democrats opposed; Harbison a Democrat → recorded against constitutional carry.

profile.background: GA State Senate District 16 (Fayette/Spalding) since 2015. One of the chamber's most outspoken Christian-conservative members; lead sponsor of the Georgia Religious Freedom Restoration Act (SB36 2025; earlier SB221), the Constitutional Carry Act (SB319 2022), and school-chaplains SB325; NRA 'A' + lifetime member; Georgia Right to Life-endorsed. Running for re-election 2026.
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-20 — evidence-scored from his authored/sponsored bills (strongest evidence tier), GA party-line marquee votes, GRTL/GLA pro-life records, and his own campaign statements. Sponsorship cells are HARD; a few party-line dispositions (HB481, SB233, HB1105) could not be individually pulled (legis.ga.gov bot-blocked) but are scored per the marquee-party-line rule given his consistent R record.

marty-harbin@GA.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06-20. Movement Christian-conservative; SPONSOR (strongest tier) on the marquee bills. Christian liberty: lead author of Georgia's RFRA (SB36 2025 / SB221) and school-chaplains SB325. Life: GRTL-endorsed + GLA-certified (2022), supported HB481 (heartbeat). Marriage: co-sponsor SB140 (minor gender-care ban) and SB390 (anti-ALA library defund). Family: supported SB233 vouchers, authored adoption-law overhaul. Elections: co-sponsor SB202 (voter ID + drop-box/private-funding restrictions). Border: anti-sanctuary HB1105 + foreign-farmland SB420 co-sponsor. 2A: sponsor of constitutional carry SB319, NRA 'A'/lifetime member. No deviations found.

0.note: Endorsed by Georgia Right to Life (no-exceptions personhood group) and self-identifies 'proud to be pro-life.'

1.note: Supported HB481 (2019 heartbeat/LIFE Act, party-line R); Georgia Life Alliance pro-life certified (2022).

3.note: No euthanasia/assisted-suicide bill or statement found (GRTL endorsement implies opposition but no specific cite).

4.note: GRTL-endorsed anti-abortion legislator; no PP/NARAL/EMILY's List money documented.

0.note: No standalone one-man/one-woman GA bill/statement located.

2.note: Co-sponsor of SB140 (2023) banning hormone therapy and gender-transition surgery for minors — affirms biological sex.

4.note: Co-sponsor of SB390 (2024) defunding GA libraries affiliated with the American Library Association — opposes LGBTQ-promotion in public institutions.

0.note: Supported SB233 (Georgia Promise Scholarship vouchers, party-line R); authored school-chaplains SB325.

1.note: Via SB140 co-sponsorship — restricts minors' gender interventions / supports parental authority on minors' medical care.

2.note: No K-12 CRT/SOGI-curriculum vote individually documented (SB390 library bill is adjacent, not K-12 curriculum).

3.note: No porn-age-verification vote individually tied to him.

0.note: Public statement: 'Our right to believe, worship and live as we choose is under attack' — commits to defending public religious profession.

1.note: LEAD SPONSOR of SB36, Georgia's Religious Freedom Restoration Act (conscience exemptions; signed into law 2025); earlier SB221.

2.note: RFRA covers conscience broadly but no compelled-speech/pronoun-mandate-specific measure — null.

3.note: Author of SB325 placing chaplains in public schools — public-square prayer/Christian presence.

2.note: Touts state income-tax cuts, but no balanced-budget-amendment position on this framing — null.

1.note: Co-sponsor of SB202 (2021) — strengthened photo-voter-ID requirements.

3.note: Co-sponsor of SB202, which restricted drop boxes and absentee/mail-in voting.

2.note: RFRA push is state-level conscience protection; no specific anti-federal-overreach vote — null.
Supported HB1105 (2024 Criminal Alien Track and Report Act) penalizing sanctuary jurisdictions (party-line R).

4.note: Co-sponsor of SB420 (2024) barring certain foreign acquisition of Georgia farmland.

0.note: SPONSOR of SB319, the Georgia Constitutional Carry Act (permitless carry, signed 2022).

profile.background: GA House District 14 (Bartow/Cartersville) since 2019; chairs House Special Rules. Former Bartow County firefighter and seven-term probate judge. Establishment Republican (Georgia Republican Assembly C/52 for 2024); authors mostly local/technical bills. Running for re-election 2026.
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-20 — evidence-scored. TRUE cells rest on individually-confirmed roll calls (SB233, SB140, HB1105 via FastDemocracy, where he is named) plus documented party-line marquee passages (HB481, SB202, SB319, HB1084, SB63) where he served as a Republican. NOT Georgia Life Alliance-certified (2024) and authors no culture-war legislation — his conservative marks are party-line floor votes, so unscored cells are left null rather than assumed.

mitchell-scoggins@GA.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06-20. Establishment Republican; conservative party-line voting record but middling independent ratings (GRA C/52) and authors no values legislation. Individually-confirmed YEA: SB233 (2024 vouchers — did NOT defect though 16+ rural Rs did), SB140 (2023 minor gender-care ban), HB1105 (2024 anti-sanctuary). Party-line R on HB481 (heartbeat), HB1084 (divisive concepts), SB202 (elections), SB63 (cash bail), SB319 (constitutional carry). Not GLA-certified; many life/marriage sub-cells left null (no documented individual position). No deviations found.

0.note: No personhood statement found; not GLA-certified (2024).

1.note: HB481 (2019 heartbeat/LIFE Act) passed House 92-78 party-line; Scoggins a sitting Republican → recorded for.

4.note: Did not complete the GLA 2024 pro-life questionnaire; no PP/NARAL money documented either way — null.

profile.background: GA House District 15 (Cartersville/Bartow) since 2019. Serves on Insurance, Appropriations, Higher Education. Establishment Republican; carries Governor's-priority bills (HB1159 farm tax exemption, HB1302 workforce). Authored HB130 (peace-officer loan repayment) and HB1480 (unemployment benefits for COVID-vaccine-mandate refusers). Running for re-election 2026.
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-20 — evidence-scored. Individually-confirmed YEA on SB233 (named in the YES list); other marquee cells (HB481, SB140, HB1084, SB36, SB202, HB1105, SB319) scored per documented party-line passage with him a sitting Republican. GLA pro-life certified (2020). Note: John Birch Society 'Freedom Index' 33% is a fiscal/limited-government metric (he carries appropriations + Governor's bills) — NOT a values-voter signal and not scored here.

matthew-gambill@GA.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06-20. Establishment Republican, GLA-certified pro-life (2020), Tabernacle Baptist member. Individually-confirmed YEA on SB233 (2024 vouchers). Party-line R on HB481 (heartbeat), SB140 (minor gender-care ban), HB1084 (divisive concepts + trans-sports), SB36 (RFRA), SB202 (elections), HB1105 (anti-sanctuary), SB319 (constitutional carry). Authored HB130 (back-the-blue peace-officer loan repayment) and HB1480 (COVID vaccine-mandate medical-freedom). Freedom Index 33% is a fiscal-libertarian metric, not a social-values deviation. No conservative deviations found.
